growth_records.strain: not identified (disintegrated granular sludge inoculum from Eerbeek UASB papermill plant, The Netherlands); community speculated Methanosaeta concilii-like
growth_records.medium: Sodium acetate mono-substrate, autogenerative high-pressure anaerobic digestion (AHPD)

up.compound: acetate (Monod max specific uptake rate k_m,ac, aceticlastic methanogenesis)
up.units: kg COD / kg COD / d (g acetate-COD per g biomass-COD per day)
up.method: MODEL-calibrated parameter (fitted to Lindeboom et al. experimental AHPD data), NOT directly measured; atmospheric-pressure literature value 8 kg COD/kg COD/d [ref 29]

sec.compound: methane (specific methanogenic yield SMY, EXPERIMENTAL, from Lindeboom et al. [22])
sec.method: experimental SMY cited from Lindeboom et al. [22]; model reproduced 153.1 (+3.3%)

product_yields.K_S_ac: 0.05 kg COD/m3 (model-calibrated half-saturation for acetate; atmospheric ref 0.15)
product_yields.SMY_experimental_L_per_kg: Exp4 158.4, Exp5 86.1, Exp6 21.5 (from Lindeboom et al. [22])

growth_records.cite: De Crescenzo, Carmen et al. Modelling of autogenerative high-pressure anaerobic digestion in a batch reactor for the production of pressurised biogas. Biotechnology for Biofuels and Bioproducts (2022). DOI:10.1186/s13068-022-02117-x. [PMC8857836]
growth_records.snippet: the values of 5.9 kg COD kg COD-1 d-1, 0.05 kg COD m-3 and 0.02 d-1 were determined, respectively... Experiments were carried out using disintegrated granular sludge as inoculum, collected from the UASB plant of Eerbeek... and sodium acetat
growth_records.notes: MODELLING paper (ADM1-style aceticlastic methanogenesis kinetic model). The max specific acetate uptake rate k_m,ac = 5.9 kg COD/kg COD/d is a MODEL-CALIBRATED parameter (is_experimental:false), fitted to the experimental AHPD batch data of Lindeboom et al. 2011 [ref 22] (sodium acetate, Eerbeek granular sludge, 0-90 bar, pH~7). Comparable atmospheric-pressure literature k_m,ac = 8, K_S = 0.15, k_dec = 0.02 [ref 29]. The specific methanogenic yields (SMY: 158.4, 86.1, 21.5 L CH4/kg for experiments 4/5/6) ARE experimental measurements but are CITED FROM Lindeboom et al. [22], not generated here. Organism not sequenced/identified; authors speculate Methanosaeta-like dominance from low K_S. No original prokaryote growth-rate measurement in this paper; mu/temperature not reported. Included for GEM relevance (acetate uptake kinetics for aceticlastic archaea) with explicit provenance caveats.
